facts.claim: Antalya is one of the largest cities in Turkey and the capital of Antalya Province, the fifth-most populous province.
Recognized as the "capital of tourism" in Turkey and a pivotal part of the Turkish Riviera, Antalya sits on Anatolia's southwest coast, flanked by the Taurus Mountains.

Antalya[a] is one of the largest cities in Turkey and the capital of Antalya Province, the fifth-most populous province. Recognized as the "capital of tourism" in Turkey and a pivotal part of the Turkish Riviera,[4] Antalya sits on Anatolia's southwest coast, flanked by the Taurus Mountains. The urban[b] population of the city is around 1.5 million, with a provincial population of 2,722,103.[5][6]
